items.rationale: Repository-local ignore patterns and generated artifacts do not affect runtime parity behavior.
Local operator guidance; not part of Hermes runtime behavior.
Contributor process guidance and release policy; no runtime behavior.
Container build/runtime path impacts deployment behavior and should be tracked for parity deltas.
Licensing and attribution metadata; no runtime effect.
Installation and operational guidance influences real-world runtime setup outcomes.
Container orchestration defaults affect gateway/runtime launch semantics and service wiring.
Container bootstrap and runtime environment export behavior affect parity at startup.
Nix packaging/install behavior affects reproducible environment parity.
Distribution packaging path impacts install parity for Homebrew users.
Upstream optional-skills blockchain tree is Python-skill-store content; Hermes Ultra keeps Rust-first runtime parity and supports external skill registries via native loader paths.
Installer behavior directly affects setup parity and bootstrap capabilities.
Gateway test deltas exercise platform adapter behavior and must be reviewed as runtime parity coverage.
Documentation-site content differs by project positioning and release policy; runtime behavior is unaffected.
Tool tests cover command, policy, and backend semantics that affect runtime parity.
CLI tests define user-facing command and setup behavior and must stay parity-reviewed.
TUI source differences affect interactive UX/runtime surfaces.
Agent tests cover model routing, provider, memory, and loop semantics.
Run-agent tests cover orchestration and provider execution semantics.
TUI package differences affect distributed interactive UI behavior.
CLI test deltas cover command contracts and user-visible runtime behavior.
Skill catalog content is curated locally while Rust runtime parity is tracked through native skill loader contracts.
Memory plugin differences affect recall/provider integration behavior.
Model-provider plugin differences affect runtime provider compatibility.
Skill catalog content is curated locally while loader and execution contracts carry runtime parity.
ACP tests cover protocol compatibility and session behavior.
Google Meet plugin differences affect connector/plugin runtime behavior.
Platform plugin differences affect adapter surface parity.
Plugin tests cover loader, hook, and runtime integration behavior.
Teams pipeline plugin differences affect platform integration behavior.
Cron tests cover scheduling/runtime behavior.
Provider tests cover model transport compatibility and error handling.
Kanban plugin differences affect plugin runtime behavior.
Observability plugin differences affect telemetry/runtime diagnostics.
Website build/generation scripts differ for local documentation publishing and do not affect Hermes runtime.
Example dashboard plugin differences affect plugin packaging and UI integration samples.
Skill catalog content is curated locally while runtime skill contracts remain tracked separately.
Skill catalog content is curated locally while runtime skill contracts remain tracked separately.
Honcho plugin tests cover memory/plugin behavior.
Skill tests cover loader, guard, and execution semantics.
Stress tests cover runtime reliability envelopes.
Website source differs for documentation/product presentation and does not change Rust runtime behavior.
Repository metadata affects text normalization and generated artifact handling, not runtime behavior.
CI workflow differences affect release and parity gate enforcement.
